scaleStep.description: Zero spacing. Use when two elements must share an edge. Always declare explicitly to communicate intent.

scaleStep.label: 4xs
scaleStep.description: Minimal spacing. Use for tight optical adjustments, such as aligning an icon to text baseline.

scaleStep.label: 3xs
scaleStep.description: Extra-small spacing. Use for the gap between an icon and its adjacent label, or between tightly coupled inline elements.

scaleStep.label: 2xs
scaleStep.description: Small spacing. Use for internal padding in compact components (badges, chips, tags) and the gap between a form label and its input.

scaleStep.label: xs
scaleStep.description: Small-medium spacing. Use for internal padding in medium-density components and the gap between related form fields.

scaleStep.label: sm
scaleStep.description: Base spacing. The default for internal component padding (buttons, inputs, cards) and the gap between sibling elements in a standard layout.

scaleStep.label: md
scaleStep.description: Large spacing. Use for padding inside containers (cards, dialogs, panels) and the gap between distinct content groups within a surface.

scaleStep.label: lg
scaleStep.description: Extra-large spacing. Use for the gap between sections within a page or between unrelated content groups.

scaleStep.label: xl
scaleStep.description: Double extra-large spacing. Use for major section breaks on desktop layouts.

scale.name: spacing-scale
scale.description: A geometric spacing scale built on a 4px base unit. Values double at regular intervals to produce a natural rhythm. Use these tokens for all padding, margin, and gap properties. The scale is intentionally constrained — if a needed value is not on the scale, reconsider the design before requesting a new token.

steps.label: sm
steps.description: The base unit. Use for default internal component padding.

principleEntry.title: Use the scale
principleEntry.description: Every spacing value in production code must reference a token from the spacing scale. Hard-coded pixel, rem, or em values are not permitted. The scale exists to constrain decisions to a set of values that are proven to produce consistent rhythm.

principleEntry.title: Density over decoration
principleEntry.description: Spacing exists to create clear relationships between elements — not to fill empty areas. Tighter spacing signals that elements are related. Wider spacing signals separation. Choose spacing based on the relationship between elements, not on what looks good in isolation.

principleEntry.title: Accessible by default
principleEntry.description: All color combinations in the system meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast requirements. AAA compliance is the goal for body text. Token pairings are pre-validated at the system level so that individual component authors do not need to manually verify contrast.

principleEntry.title: Functional first
principleEntry.description: Color choices serve functional needs — status, hierarchy, interaction — before aesthetic ones. Every color in the system has a semantic purpose. Decorative use of color is secondary to its communicative role.

style.name: spacing
style.description: A spatial system built on a 4px base unit. Defines the scale and rules for all whitespace, padding, margin, and gap values across the system. Eliminates ad hoc pixel values and reduces visual inconsistency caused by individual interpretation of spatial relationships.
style.summary: A constrained spacing scale built on a 4px base unit.

items.title: Use the scale
items.description: Every spacing value must reference a token from the spacing scale. No hard-coded values.

items.title: Density over decoration
items.description: Spacing exists to create clear relationships between elements, not to fill empty areas.

items.title: Consistent internal, flexible external
items.description: Internal spacing within a component is fixed by the component's design specification. External spacing between components is determined by the layout context.

guidelines.description: The token group that implements the spacing system.

references.name: spacing
references.role: Provides all spatial tokens from space-0 (0px) through space-8 (48px), built on a 4px base unit.

guidelines.name: spacing-scale
guidelines.description: A geometric spacing scale based on a 4px unit.

items.guidance: Use the spacing scale tokens for all padding, margin, and gap properties. Do not use hard-coded values.
items.rationale: Hard-coded values create visual inconsistency and are not responsive to system-wide spacing changes. Tokens enable global adjustment of spatial density from a single source of truth.

items.guidance: Components must not apply external margin. Margin between components is the responsibility of the parent layout.
items.rationale: Components that own their margins create unpredictable spacing when composed. A button with built-in margin-bottom behaves differently depending on what follows it. Delegating margin to layout makes spacing composable and predictable.

items.guidance: Select spacing based on the relationship between elements, not their size. Related elements use smaller spacing. Unrelated elements use larger spacing.
items.rationale: Gestalt proximity principle: objects that are closer together are perceived as related. Spacing encodes information hierarchy. A label 4px from its input is clearly associated with it. A label 32px from an input appears disconnected.

items.guidance: Do not use spacing tokens for non-spatial properties such as border-width, font-size, or icon-size. Use the tokens designated for those properties.
items.rationale: Spacing tokens are tuned for whitespace. A spacing scale step that works well as padding produces incorrect results as a border-width or icon-size. Using the wrong token category couples unrelated visual properties.

items.guidance: Use CSS gap (in Flexbox or Grid) as the primary mechanism for spacing between sibling elements. Reserve margin for spacing between non-sibling elements or layout-level offset.
items.rationale: Gap applies spacing uniformly between children without affecting the first or last child. Margin requires :first-child/:last-child overrides to prevent unwanted space at container edges.

items.guidance: For responsive layouts, reduce spacing density at smaller breakpoints by stepping down the scale. Do not introduce arbitrary breakpoint-specific values.
items.rationale: Stepping down the scale (e.g., space-7 at desktop becoming space-5 at mobile) maintains proportional relationships while conserving space. Arbitrary values break out of the scale and undermine system consistency.

items.guidance: Ensure all interactive elements maintain a minimum 44x44 CSS pixel touch target, inclusive of padding.
items.rationale: Users with motor impairments and touch device users require a minimum target size to interact reliably. WCAG 2.5.8 requires 24x24px minimum and recommends 44x44px. The spacing system's role is to ensure padding contributes to meeting this target.

items.guidance: When users increase text size to 200% via browser settings, spacing must not collapse to zero or cause content to overlap.
items.rationale: Users with low vision enlarge text. If spacing is defined in fixed pixel units that do not scale, text enlargement causes overlap and loss of content. Use rem-based spacing tokens where possible, and test all layouts at 200% text zoom.

whenToUse.description: When defining padding, margin, or gap values for any layout or component. All spatial values in production code must reference the spacing scale.
When establishing vertical rhythm between content sections, form groups, or card layouts.
When setting internal padding for containers such as cards, dialogs, panels, and page regions.
When controlling the gap between sibling elements in Flexbox or Grid layouts.

whenNotToUse.description: When defining border widths, outline offsets, or stroke values.

alternative.name: border-width
alternative.rationale: Border tokens are tuned for visual weight at sub-pixel and single-pixel sizes. Spacing tokens start at 0px and jump to values optimized for whitespace, which produce incorrect visual results when applied to borders.

whenNotToUse.description: When setting font sizes or line heights.

alternative.name: typography
alternative.rationale: Typographic tokens follow a modular scale designed for readability and vertical rhythm. Spacing tokens follow a geometric scale designed for whitespace. The two scales serve different purposes and should not be interchanged.

whenNotToUse.description: When sizing icons or illustration containers.

alternative.name: icon-size
alternative.rationale: Icon size tokens are calibrated to optical alignment with adjacent text at each type scale step. Spacing tokens do not account for optical sizing and produce misaligned icons.
